title: Impossible Travel: Domestic to International CNP Burst Within 60 Minutes
narrative: Cardholder completes legitimate in-store chip transaction in Des Moines, Iowa at 14:22 UTC, followed by online purchase from London-based merchant at 14:48 UTC (26 minutes later), then Singapore digital goods vendor at 15:03 UTC. Physical distance exceeds 8,000 miles between first and final transaction with zero possibility of cardholder travel. Fraudster obtained card details through skimming or data breach, then monetizes through high-margin CNP channels before card block. Domestic transaction shows normal addr1 consistency and dist1 under 50km, while international transactions exhibit mismatched P_emaildomain (suspicious temporary email services), elevated card1_amt_zscore above 3.5, and ProductCD shift from physical goods to digital/services. Pattern peaks during US business hours when cardholders less likely monitoring accounts.
indicators: Geographic impossibility: 8,000+ mile distance between transactions within 60-minute window
Channel switch: chip-verified in-store followed by two CNP transactions
P_emaildomain changes from established provider to disposable/temporary email service
card1_amt_zscore spikes above 3.5 for international transactions versus cardholder baseline
dist1 jumps from <50km (domestic) to null/inconsistent for cross-border transactions
ProductCD shifts from 'W' (physical) to 'C'/'H' (digital goods/services) for international purchases
International merchants in high-risk MCCs: 5816 (digital games), 5734 (software), 4816 (telecom)
summary: A legitimate chip transaction in one country is followed within 60 minutes by card-not-present purchases from merchants 8,000+ miles away in different countries, with email addresses switching to temporary services and transaction amounts spiking above the cardholder's normal baseline.
reasoning: The cardholder cannot physically travel thousands of miles in under an hour, so the international online purchases represent a fraudster using stolen card details to quickly buy high-value digital goods that can be resold before the card is blocked.
indicator_explanations: Card appears in locations thousands of miles apart impossibly fast
Legitimate in-person purchase immediately followed by remote online transactions
Email switches from trusted provider to throwaway disposable service
International purchase amounts wildly exceed this cardholder's normal spending
Distance tracking breaks down as transactions cross international borders
Card pivots from buying physical goods to intangible digital products
International merchants sell high-risk digital goods frequently targeted by fraudsters
